nodes.label: AI as coding assistant for unfamiliar languages
nodes.grounding_span: how I can word a question with casual language which is not precise or perfectly correct but the AI understands

nodes.label: AI as cognitive flow maintainer
nodes.label_negative: Web search causing frustration and flow loss
nodes.grounding_span: prevents losing the 'flow state' that is easy to lose when searching the web

nodes.label: AI accuracy in numerical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: focus only on smaller, precise issues

nodes.label: AI output quality and appropriateness
nodes.grounding_span: it refuses to change its code/output even though I have pointed out it is incorrect

nodes.label: AI as threat to engaging manual work
nodes.label_negative: AI as insufficient for secure code
nodes.grounding_span: it is a threat to software engineers but I don't think it will be capable of delivering well-structured but more importantly secure code

nodes.label: AI use sanctioned by workplace
nodes.grounding_span: We do not input our own code into the models

nodes.label: Code quality and correctness
nodes.grounding_span: well-structured but more importantly secure code

nodes.label: Skill preservation and professional adaptability
nodes.grounding_span: maintain my ability to write good code

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: It has made me feel satisfied when I have been struggling on a problem for a while and it immediately gives me clarity

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: It has been frustrating sometimes though when it refuses to change its code/output even though I have pointed out it is incorrect

nodes.label: Concern about AI replacing skills
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think it will be capable of delivering well-structured but more importantly secure code for quite a long time

nodes.label: Compliance with workplace AI discouragement
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think we will be allowed to put our code in it anytime soon, but I don't mind that

nodes.label: Adaptive interaction based on output quality
nodes.grounding_span: if I look at the output of the model and it immediately makes more sense to me what I have to do then I will just use the first output

nodes.label: Preference for maintaining control over architecture
nodes.grounding_span: I like to structure my code to fit my work's repo and style
